{"society_power": 4.5, "state_power": 2.5, "society_change": 0.5, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Federalist decentralization and liberal reforms weaken the center", "reasoning": "Broad male suffrage (1853), abolition of slavery, and vibrant artisan/party mobilization expanded participatory society, while federalist devolution (culminating in the 1858 Confederation), recurring civil wars, and the Melo coup further fragmented an already weak central state."}
